§6Short-Lived Nuclear Waste§f

§aFission products from spent fuel that decay into useful materials — 7 sub-classes of waste with different outputs!§f

§bShort-Lived Nuclear Waste§f is a class of waste with a relatively short estimated half-life, usually because the constituent radioisotopes are fission products. There are §e7 sub-classes§f, one for each source element: U-233, U-235, Np-237, Pu-239, Pu-240, Pu-241, and Sa-326 (schrabidium).

§6Production§f: Extracted from spent fuel using a §bSILEX§f (Separation of Isotopes by Laser EXcitation). Each fuel type yields its corresponding waste sub-class.

§6Non-Decayed Recycling§f (per type, §bSILEX§f or §bNuclear Waste Disposal Drum§f):
§b• U-233§f → §e4% Pu-238§f, §e12% Sr-90§f, §e10% I-131§f, §e14% Cs-137§f, §e60% generic waste
§b• U-235§f → §e12% Pu-238§f, §e10% Sr-90§f, §e10% I-131§f, §e12% Cs-137§f, §e56% generic waste
§b• Np-237§f → §e40% Pu-238§f, §e7% Sr-90§f, §e5% I-131§f, §e8% Cs-137§f, §e40% generic waste
§b• Pu-239§f → §e9.9% Pu-240§f, §e24.7% Pu-241§f, §e1.9% Sr-90§f, §e4.9% I-131§f, §e5.9% Cs-137§f, §e52.4% generic waste
§b• Pu-240§f → §e15% Pu-241§f, §e5% Np-237§f, §e2% Sr-90§f, §e5% I-131§f, §e7% Cs-137§f, §e66% generic waste
§b• Pu-241§f → §e25% Am-241§f, §e35% Am-242m§f, §e5% Tc-99§f, §e3% I-131§f, §e7% Cs-137§f, §e25% generic waste
§b• Sa-326§f → §e7% Pb-209§f, §e7% Au-198§f, §e5% Cs-137§f, §e5% I-131§f, §e76% generic waste

§6Decayed Recycling§f (after decay in Nuclear Waste Disposal Drum):
Non-decayed yields become Zirconium, Dust, Lead, Bismuth, and Uranium-238 in various proportions depending on source.

§c⚠ Safety§f: Pyrophoric when not decayed! Handle with care.

§eTip§f: Americium-242m type is currently unobtainable. The §bNuclear Waste Disposal Drum§f is your primary tool for cycling short-lived waste into useful materials.
